suggestions.title: I met Chairman Jensen Huang in Las Vegas
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today's video is a bit like a vlog from CES in Las Vegas. The highlight is, of course, NVIDIA CEO/Chairman Jensen Huang, and he's incredibly cool. Since there's no food in this video, I'm personally curious to see how many views it will get.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewYork #Ne

suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited the Las Vegas branch of Gordon Ramsay Burger, which is far from being a good value. Following Hell's Kitchen and the Gordon Ramsay Steakhouse, this is the burger. While it's delicious, it's also incredibly expensive.

suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I had pizza (apizza) in New Haven, Connecticut. It's officially recognized by the U.S. Congress as the Pizza Capital of the United States. I was surprised, but it's true.

TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

suggestions.title: I couldn't afford to go to a hospital in New York
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I went to the dentist in New York and was recommended some ridiculous 30 million won treatment, so I immediately went to a friend's dentist and got it done. These American doctors who try to force root canals and crowns on perfectly healthy teeth are truly vile.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok:

suggestions.title: What Happens When You Order a $40 Soup at a New York Ski Resort
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I ordered 40,000 won worth of soup and fries at Hunter Mountain Ski Resort in New York. Things like this are expensive in the US, but it was so cold outside that I enjoyed it.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewYork #NewYorkRestaurants #NewYorkVlog #NewYorkMeals

suggestions.title: The world's cheapest Michelin 3-star restaurant
suggestions.descriptionSnippet: Today, I visited Sushi Sho in New York City, the world's most affordable Michelin 3-star restaurant. Of the 15 3-star restaurants worldwide, it's the only one in the US and the only one outside of Japan. The menu is omakase sushi, and they offer afternoon chirashi and chirashi takeout only on Friday

suggestions.title: "Surviving China is proof of strength."
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried Molly Tea, one of China's leading milk tea brands. This is my third introduction, following Hey Tea (Hee Tea) and Chaji (Pae Wang Cha Hee). I tried it in New York's Chinatown. It's famous for its jasmine tea.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewYork #NewYorkRestaurants #

suggestions.title: The realistic size of the American-style single-serving burger set
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited a famous burger joint in Austin, Texas. They're called Hopdoddy and they use local Texas beef, Wagyu, and other ingredients. Everything from the burgers to the sides, like the sweet potato fries and baby cabbage, is delicious. However, tipping is a bit tricky.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins

suggestions.title: The American diet makes Asian pancreases sick.
suggestions.descriptionSnippet: Today, I was in Austin, Texas, and after eating a ton of barbecue, I decided I couldn't eat any more, so I decided to eat healthy. suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited Terry Black BBQ, a Texas barbecue mecca in Austin, Texas. It's similar to Franklin BBQ, a nearby restaurant that Meat Man recently visited, but it's much larger and has a shorter wait.

suggestions.descriptionSnippet:

suggestions.title: Tesla's self-driving taxis are currently in operation.
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I rode a Tesla robotaxi, a self-driving taxi service currently in operation. It's only available in Austin, Texas, and the Bay Area, California, and it's amazing. It's a completely different experience from Google's Waymo or Amazon's Zoox.

suggestions.title: Despite the name
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried Japan's famous Ohayo Jersey Milk Pudding again. I tried the regular milk flavor before, but this time I tried it with a caffe latte (coffee) flavor. Specifically, it's a pudding made with milk from cows from the British Isle of Jersey.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins

suggestions.title: Traditional Japanese home cooking that even Japanese people don't eat because it's expensive
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried obanzai in Kyoto, Japan. It's a traditional home-cooked meal with seasonal ingredients, and it's delicious. It's called Nakashimaya.

TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

suggestions.title: What Korea definitely does better than Japan
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried taiyaki, a winter street food in Osaka, Japan. Also known as taiyaki, it's the originator of Korean bungeoppang (fish-shaped bread) and ingeoppang (fish-shaped bread). It's a bit pricier than in Korea.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewYork #NewYorkRestaurants #NewYork

suggestions.title: Gyukatsu
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, we're visiting Kyoto Katsugyu Honten, a famous gyukatsu restaurant in Kyoto. Here, you can enjoy real sirloin, tenderloin, and other cuts, not molded meat.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewYork #NewYorkRestaurants #NewYorkVlog #NewYorkMeals

suggestions.title: A cost-effective chain store that makes noodles directly in Japan
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I ordered "Sanuki Udon," one of Japan's three most famous udon noodles. It's from Marugame Seimen, a restaurant that also has a Korean branch, and the noodles are hand-made on-site, making them incredibly delicious.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewYork #NewYorkRestaurants #N

suggestions.title: What happens when you order lobster on the streets of New York?
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried the Bryant Park Lobster Butter Corn Sandwich, arguably the most expensive street food in New York. Think of it as a lobster roll, but a small cone costs a whopping 51,000 won.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

suggestions.title: New York chicken skewers that have completely lost their original intentions...
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried the signature gourmet chicken skewers at Bryant Park in New York City. They're famous for their chicken portraits and their annual price increases, but this year, the gourmet chicken skewers were surprisingly unavailable. Oh, and they're still expensive.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok:

suggestions.title: American Street Food Bomb Toast (Hope Edition)
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I had a Texas BBQ grilled cheese sandwich at Bryant Park in New York. As expected from a Christmas market, it was on the pricey side, but it was still one of the most delicious meals I've had at the park, and the food was hot, which was nice.

TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

suggestions.title: Expensive but Delicious New York Street Food (Hope Edition)
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried tomato basil soup at Bryant Park in New York. It was a bit pricey because it was a winter Christmas market, but the long line and crowds made it a delicious meal.

suggestions.title: American street food
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried street food in Bryant Park, New York, a place known for its endocrine-disrupting properties. They even boiled the entire dish in plastic wrap. The food was meatballs.

suggestions.title: The latest news on the 2-hour wait for Bungeoppang
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited Namyeong Station's yingeoppang, the best red bean bungeoppang in Korea, after a long time. It used to be a street vendor, but now they have their own store, and the price has gone up a bit: 3 for 2,000 won. Still, it's absolutely delicious.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tikt

suggestions.title: The latest news in Seoul: Fake(?) salt bread is priced at 6
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited Artist Bakery, a salt bread specialty store run by the London Bagel Museum, a famous bakery in Seoul. The wait can be a bit long, but they have a variety of breads. However, the prices are a bit on the high side.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

Today's feature is Gwanghwamun Gukbap, one of only six Michelin-starred restaurants in Korea. Located in Gwanghwamun, it serves dishes like pork gomtang (pork bone soup) and boiled pork. They're delicious.

suggestions.title: Food that was delicious when I was young
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited Grandma Mabokrim's Tteokbokki, the most famous tteokbokki place in Sindangdong. They're famous for their instant tteokbokki.

Today's stop is Susu Hotteok, located next to Seokchon Lake in Jamsil. In the summer, they sell sweet corn, and in the winter, they sell hotteok. It's only 1,500 won, but it's incredibly filling and delicious. I recommend it as a winter snack instead of bungeoppang (fish-shaped bread).

suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited Cheongwaok, a sundae soup restaurant near Seokchon Lake in Jamsil. While sundae soup is now priced at 12,000 won, the broth is clean, odorless, and quite tasty.

suggestions.title: A common American snack: fried pickles
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried fried pickles, a dish Americans love but Asians find incomprehensible. They sold pickles almost like kimchi restaurants in Korea, and they cost 21,500 won.

suggestions.title: New York hot dog (21
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried one of Bryant Park's signature hot dogs. It's K-style, with fries on the side, and I had a half-and-half mozzarella. Oh, and the price is now 21,000 won, probably due to the exchange rate fluctuating every year.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins

suggestions.title: Instant hand-made noodles that Koreans would love
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried hand-pulled noodles at Bryant Park in New York City. Also known as "Biang Biang Men" or "Yu Po Men," it's a Chinese Xi'an-style hand-pulled noodle dish. This time, though, it's the despair side.

suggestions.title: American-style baked potatoes with butter
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:

suggestions.title: Bryant Park Returns 2025-26 (Hope Edition)
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today's video is the 2025-26 Bryant Park return/comeback video. Haha. I've only shown you despair episodes so far, but this one is a hopeful one, full of fun and winter vibes. I went ice skating, explored the shops, and even had some hot chocolate.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

#NewY

suggestions.title: American Saemaeul-ho(?) train business class
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I took an Amtrak local train from Washington, D.C. to New York. It was a much slower regional train, the Palmetto, than the Acela I took last time. You could say it was on par with the Saemaeul-ho. I also bought a salad in the cafe car. The burgers and hot dogs looked stunning.

Instagram: @N

suggestions.descriptionSnippet: Today, I visited Chef Edward Lee's restaurant, Succotash Prime. It's located in Washington, D.C., and it's not a Korean restaurant, but a Southern-style restaurant. I had the spicy chili paste-based marinated chicken and pork belly wrap, but honestly, the taste and quality were disappointing for the

suggestions.title: I tried room service at the world's best hotel.
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried breakfast room service at the Four Seasons Hotel in Washington, DC, one of the world's best hotel chains. I had an omelet and a vegetable smoothie. The room was really nice, and it was a wonderful experience.

Today, I visited a steakhouse right next to the White House in Washington, D.C., the capital of the United States. I had the American-style ribeye, and it was delicious, as expected from a capitalist country.

suggestions.title: KTX(?) train business class in the US
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I rode the Amtrak Acela, America's KTX. Last time, I showed you the train from New York to Boston, and this time, it's from New York to Washington, D.C. I also enjoyed a delicious cheese tray snack. The train tickets were a bit pricey, though.

suggestions.title: America is happy because it doesn't care about health.
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I tried American specialties at an American orchard and apple farm. Things like apple cider donuts, chicken tenders, and a turkey sandwich. Haha. They're not healthy at all, but they're delicious.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok

suggestions.title: The scale of American farms rivals that of large supermarkets.
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, we went to an apple orchard, one of the most common outdoor activities and traditional games in the US. We picked apples, picked vegetables, and bought snacks. There were a variety of apples, and we had a great time near New York in the northeastern US.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.t

suggestions.title: A steakhouse frequented by New York's financial community
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited Smith & Wollensky in New York City. It's famous as the restaurant where Warren Buffett hosts his charity auction luncheon and also appeared in the film "The Devil Wears Prada." More importantly, it's said to be frequented by local Wall Street financial professionals.

Instagram: @NY

suggested.address: Smith & Wollensky, 797, 3rd Avenue, Turtle Bay, Manhattan Community Board 6, Manhattan, New York County, New York, 10022, United States

suggestions.title: If you like New York pizza
suggestions.descriptionSnippet:
Today, I visited L'industrie Pizza, a local favorite in New York. Best known for its burrata, fig bacon pizza, and truffle pizza, it's no exaggeration to say it's currently the most popular pizza place in New York.

Instagram: @NYeat.ins
TikTok: @NYeat.tiktok
